title: podman image scp: implement --quiet
body:
Relay --quiet to save & load commands, in both Rootless
and Rootful transfer functions.

Also, a little cleanup:
- remove unuseful SOURCE/DEST printfs
- refactor duplication in execMachine()
- fix Debug("Executing") statements to include the actual
  command they're executing

Signed-off-by: Charlie Doern <cdoern@redhat.com>
Signed-off-by: Ed Santiago <santiago@redhat.com>

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *edsantiago* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [edsantiago] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

And now, a word from our sponsor: **we need to get rid of machinectl**. It is a bad bad idea:
```console
# machinectl shell -q esm@.host /usr/bin/false;echo $?

0
```
That is: (1) it always spits out a useless unwanted blank line; and (2) it does not propagate exit status! This is actually documented:
```
        Note that machinectl shell does not propagate the exit code/status
        of the invoked shell process. Use systemd-run instead if that
        information is required (see below).
```
This PR does not intend to address that. My goal here is simply to make `-q` work (not great, because of the empty machinectl line, but better than it was) and to do a little maintenance cleanup. I cannot fix the underlying machinectl problem because I don't know why it was used in the first place. I will leave it to others to follow up with a better fix. I just don't want to carry all these diffs in #12797 any more.

I'm confused: CI is green, as in all green, ~~even the 'Total Success' step~~ and there's no `tide` step which is always always yellow.  @cevich has something changed in the CI world?

(I see two recent changes to `.cirrus.yml`, both from Monday, neither looks like it could cause this)

(UPDATE: I meant `tide`, not Total Success. `tide` is the one that forces everything to yellow. What happened to `tide`?)

> What happened to `tide`

The way that's integrated into the PR workflow involves entirely external entities (configuration and persistent processes) - unlike cirrus with it's embedded YAML file.  So if `tide` breaks, it's always for external (to the repo) reasons - like the process stopped running and/or it's configuration broke. So there's literally nothing you or I can do about it, but (for future reference) Brent knows whom to poke for status, if there's ever an extended outage.
